title: Event Operations & Registration Platform

images.alt: Event Operations Platform — Bengaluru Tech Summit registration and admin dashboard

overview.description: A multi-event Django platform for large-scale tech summits — configurable registration, exhibitor lifecycle, and unified ticketing at scale.
overview.details:
Production-grade event operations platform built for Bengaluru Tech Summit, designed to run end-to-end operations for large technology exhibitions and conferences. It handles exhibitor and startup booth applications, sponsorship, visitor and delegate registration, payments, floor/booth allocation, and operational reporting — all from a single admin-controlled system.

What makes it stand out is its highly configurable registration architecture. The same platform supports multiple registration paradigms side by side: legacy authenticated exhibitor flows, guest-first public registrations (Startup Zone, Poster, Elevate, Visa Clearance), and a config-first unified ticketing system where categories, pricing, sale windows, day access, promo codes, and form fields are all admin-configurable — no code changes required per event. Super-admins can spin up and configure new events through the admin panel, making the system reusable across different summits and formats.

Built for event organizers and ops teams managing large-scale technology summits and trade exhibitions — exhibitors applying for booth space, startups in dedicated zones, delegates purchasing conference passes, sponsors with association-specific pricing, and admin staff running approvals, payments, floor allocation, and analytics, all configurable per event.

features: Multi-event configuration — Super-admin CRUD for events, sectors, organization types, and per-event ticket setup
Config-first unified ticketing — Admin-defined categories, subcategories, ticket types, early-bird pricing, inventory, and day-access rules; public discovery at slug-based URLs (/tickets/{eventSlug})
Dynamic form builder — Versioned form field configuration with field groups and JSON validation rules per registration type
Guest-first delegate registration — Single-page flow with unlimited delegates per company, GST auto-fill, group discounts, and UTM/source tracking
Separate delegate identity domain — High-volume attendees in a dedicated contacts/delegates model with OTP/magic-link auth and a full Delegate Panel for badges, receipts, and upgrades
Exhibitor application lifecycle — Multi-step onboarding, admin approve/reject/hold, booth allocation (shell scheme / raw space), pass auto-allocation by SQM, and co-exhibitor sub-accounts
Startup Zone & public registration flows — No-login session-based drafts with auto-save, association promocode pricing, and encrypted GST/PAN handling
Dual payment gateway integration — CCAvenue (INR) and PayPal (USD/EUR) with webhooks, partial payments, TDS/surcharge handling, and receipt verification
Admin operations suite — Registration analytics and Excel exports, soft-delete with restore, payment dashboards, floor/booth management, and delegate push notifications
External integrations — Email delivery, GST lookup API, attendee sync, QR codes, PDF receipts, and CRM sync
Config-first ticketing architecture — Event behavior (auth policy, selection mode, receipt patterns) stored in database config; catalog hierarchy fully admin-driven
Snapshot-based commerce — Price and day-access captured at purchase time, preserving audit integrity when catalog prices change
Identity separation for scale — Portal users (exhibitors/admins) and high-volume ticket delegates in separate models with dedicated auth middleware and custom user backends
Multi-flow pattern reuse — Shared auto-save drafts, GST lookup, promocode validation, and preview → submit → invoice → gateway → confirmation pipeline
Role-based access control — Granular permissions for super-admin, admin, exhibitor, co-exhibitor, sponsor, sales, visitor, and floor-management roles
Service-oriented architecture — Payment, ticketing, and receipt logic isolated in Django service modules, keeping views thin and business logic testable
